pi_items.source: README.md line 68 claims 'inverted_colors.png', but export_invert.sh line 27 exports 'inverted_image' and verifier.py lines 216-222 searches for 'inverted_image.jpg'
Standard RGB color inversion formula verified via web search and mathematical validation. Formula: (R', G', B') = (255-R, 255-G, 255-B). This is the standard used in image editing software like GIMP.
install_gimp.sh installs GIMP, setup_gimp.sh configures GIMP 2.10 directories (/home/ga/.config/GIMP/2.10)
README.md line 22 and 48. Verified through GIMP 2.10 documentation - this is the standard menu location for color inversion.
verifier.py lines 87, 126. README.md line 100. SSIM 0.95 is a standard threshold used in industry (SPEC benchmarks use 0.95-0.90 range) for near-perfect image similarity.
README.md line 111, verifier.py line 292. Calculation: 75% = 3 out of 4 criteria met. Score calculation: (criteria_met / 4) * 100 >= 75
verifier.py line 268: total_criteria = 4. README.md lines 100-103 lists the 4 criteria: perfect inversion match, significant color changes, histogram inversion, image modified from original

pi_items.metadata_value: Significant color changes (>70% of pixels changed significantly)
pi_items.verified_value: Significant color changes (>70% of pixels changed significantly)

pi_items.source: setup_invert_task.sh lines 16-28. If both Unsplash downloads fail, script creates synthetic colorful image using ImageMagick convert command with red, green, blue, yellow, cyan, magenta, black rectangles and text
setup_invert_task.sh line 12, line 35. Image is downloaded/created to this location

privileged_info_summary: The color_invert@1 task requires GIMP 2.10 to apply the standard RGB color inversion to an image via the Colors → Invert menu. The RGB inversion formula (255-R, 255-G, 255-B) is the industry-standard mathematical transformation verified in GIMP documentation. The task sources a colorful image from Unsplash (photo-1506905925346-21bda4d32df4 or fallback photo-1441974231531-c6227db76b6e) to /home/ga/Desktop/original_photo.jpg, with synthetic image generation as final fallback using ImageMagick. Verification uses SSIM ≥ 0.95 threshold (verified as industry standard) comparing against mathematically perfect inversion, requiring 3 of 4 criteria (75% score) to pass: perfect SSIM match, >70% pixels changed significantly, histogram correlation >0.7, and image modified from original. CRITICAL DISCREPANCY: README documentation incorrectly claims export filename is 'inverted_colors.png', but actual implementation (export_invert.sh, verifier.py) uses 'inverted_image.jpg'. Task timeout is 120 seconds with max 10 steps.
